question: When and where is Global Tipping Points Conference 2026?
answer: GTP 2026 runs 12–15 October 2026 in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. The conference is hosted by the Sunway Centre for Planetary Health and partners. Exact venue details and daily timings will be confirmed closer to the event—check the programme page and your registration communications for updates.

question: Who should attend?
answer: The programme is designed for researchers, policymakers, practitioners, funders, and partners working on climate, health, finance, culture, and systems change. Whether you focus on science, implementation, or mobilising action, you will find sessions that connect evidence to decisions at scale.

question: How do I register?
answer: Use the Register page on this site to complete your registration. If you need an invoice, group booking, or other arrangement, contact the team via the Get involved page (Contact section) and we will guide you.

question: What is included in registration?
answer: What is included depends on the ticket or pass type you select. In general, registration covers access to the core conference programme and materials as described at the point of purchase. Any pre-conference workshops, side events, or networking programmes will be called out separately if they need an add-on or invitation.

question: How can I submit an abstract or workshop proposal?
answer: Submissions for abstracts and action workshops are handled through the Submissions page (/events/gtp-2026/submissions). Please read the pillar and theme guidance, word limits, and deadlines on that page before you submit. If the form or process is unclear, use the contact options on Get involved.

question: Will sessions be recorded or hybrid?
answer: We aim to share selected content where speakers and rights allow. A final policy on recording, live streaming, and on-demand access will be published ahead of the conference. Register to receive official updates so you do not miss changes to format or access.

question: Travel, visas, and getting to Kuala Lumpur
answer: Kuala Lumpur is well connected by air. Visa rules depend on your nationality; check the latest requirements with Malaysian immigration or your embassy well in advance. We recommend booking flexible travel where possible and allowing extra time for connections. Practical travel tips and recommended areas to stay will be shared with registered participants.

question: Accessibility, dietary needs, and inclusion
answer: We want everyone to participate fully. When you register, share accessibility requirements, dietary needs, or inclusion considerations so we can plan reasonable support with the venue. For anything sensitive or complex, contact us directly via Get involved and we will work with you confidentially.

question: Code of conduct and respectful participation
answer: GTP 2026 is a professional meeting built on respect, curiosity, and collaboration. Harassment, discrimination, or disruptive behaviour is not tolerated. If you experience or witness a problem, please reach out to conference staff or use the contact routes on the Get involved page so we can respond quickly.

question: Who can I contact for partnerships or media enquiries?
answer: For partnerships, media, or general questions not answered here, use the Contact section on the Get involved page (/events/gtp-2026/get-involved#contact). Please include your organisation, role, and what you need—we will route your message to the right team.
